---
title: "The Affordability Squeeze"
subtitle: "How Rising Costs Have Outpaced Income Across American Counties (2009–2023)"
author: "Alysha Nadeem, Camden Davis, Gerrit Mitchell, Thrisha Jawahar"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/dilernia/STA418-518
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggthemes)
library(viridis)
library(patchwork)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(leaflet)
library(plotly)
library(tigris)
library(sf)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# Load data
census_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dilernia/STA418-518/main/Data/census_data_county_2009-2023.csv"
cpi_url    <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dilernia/STA418-518/refs/heads/main/Data/cpi_2000-2024.csv"

census_raw <- read_csv(census_url, show_col_types = FALSE)
cpi_raw    <- read_csv(cpi_url,    show_col_types = FALSE)

# Clean census
census_clean <- census_raw |>
  clean_names() |>
  mutate(
    geoid        = str_pad(as.character(geoid), width = 5, side = "left", pad = "0"),
    county_state = str_squish(as.character(county_state)),
    year         = as.integer(year),
    population   = as.numeric(population),
    median_income             = as.numeric(median_income),
    median_monthly_rent_cost  = as.numeric(median_monthly_rent_cost),
    median_monthly_home_cost  = as.numeric(median_monthly_home_cost),
    prop_female  = as.numeric(prop_female),
    prop_male    = as.numeric(prop_male),
    prop_poverty = as.numeric(prop_poverty)
  ) |>
  distinct() |>
  filter(year >= 2009, year <= 2023) |>
  filter(
    is.na(population)               | population >= 0,
    is.na(median_income)            | median_income >= 0,
    is.na(median_monthly_rent_cost) | median_monthly_rent_cost >= 0,
    is.na(median_monthly_home_cost) | median_monthly_home_cost >= 0,
    is.na(prop_female)  | between(prop_female,  0, 1),
    is.na(prop_male)    | between(prop_male,    0, 1),
    is.na(prop_poverty) | between(prop_poverty, 0, 1)
  )

# Clean CPI
cpi_clean <- cpi_raw |>
  clean_names() |>
  mutate(
    year        = as.integer(year),
    period      = as.character(period),
    period_name = str_squish(as.character(period_name)),
    series_id   = as.character(series_id),
    date        = as.character(date),
    cpi         = as.numeric(cpi)
  ) |>
  distinct()

cpi_yearly <- cpi_clean |>
  filter(str_detect(period, "^M\\d{2}$")) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(
    date_based_data = min(ymd(date), na.rm = TRUE),
    annual_avg_cpi  = mean(cpi, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Merge and derive
cpi_2024 <- cpi_yearly |> filter(year == 2024) |> pull(annual_avg_cpi)

county_fused <- census_clean |>
  left_join(cpi_yearly, by = "year") |>
  mutate(
    median_income_2024_dollars             = median_income * (cpi_2024 / annual_avg_cpi),
    median_monthly_rent_cost_2024_dollars  = median_monthly_rent_cost * (cpi_2024 / annual_avg_cpi),
    median_monthly_home_cost_2024_dollars  = median_monthly_home_cost * (cpi_2024 / annual_avg_cpi),
    poverty_tier = ntile(prop_poverty, 4),
    poverty_tier = factor(poverty_tier,
      levels = 1:4,
      labels = c("Low Poverty", "Medium-Low", "Medium-High", "High Poverty")
    ),
    state        = str_extract(county_state, "[^,]+$") |> str_squish(),
    county_name  = str_extract(county_state, "^[^,]+") |> str_squish(),
    rent_to_income_ratio = median_monthly_rent_cost / (median_income / 12)
  )

# Value box calculations
cpi_2009 <- cpi_yearly |> filter(year == 2009) |> pull(annual_avg_cpi)
cpi_2023 <- cpi_yearly |> filter(year == 2023) |> pull(annual_avg_cpi)
cpi_change_pct <- round((cpi_2023 - cpi_2009) / cpi_2009 * 100, 1)

income_change <- county_fused |>
  filter(year %in% c(2009, 2023), !is.na(median_income_2024_dollars)) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(avg = mean(median_income_2024_dollars, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  summarise(change = diff(avg)) |>
  pull(change)

avg_burden_2023 <- county_fused |>
  filter(year == 2023, !is.na(rent_to_income_ratio)) |>
  summarise(avg = mean(rent_to_income_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  pull(avg)

pct_burdened <- county_fused |>
  filter(year == 2023, !is.na(rent_to_income_ratio)) |>
  summarise(pct = mean(rent_to_income_ratio > 0.30, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  pull(pct)

# Map data
county_shape <- tigris::counties(cb = TRUE, year = 2023, class = "sf")
map_data_2023 <- county_fused |> filter(year == 2023)

gg_map_2023 <- county_shape |>
  left_join(map_data_2023, by = c("GEOID" = "geoid")) |>
  filter(STUSPS %in% state.abb) |>
  tigris::shift_geometry() |>
  st_cast("MULTIPOLYGON") |>
  st_transform(crs = 4326) |>
  st_simplify(dTolerance = 0.01, preserveTopology = TRUE)

pal_poverty <- colorNumeric(
  palette  = "YlOrRd",
  domain   = gg_map_2023$prop_poverty,
  na.color = "white"
)

# Income trend data
tier_colors <- c(
  "Low Poverty"  = "#1565C0",
  "Medium-Low"   = "#42A5F5",
  "Medium-High"  = "#EF6C00",
  "High Poverty" = "#B71C1C"
)

income_trend <- county_fused |>
  filter(!is.na(median_income_2024_dollars), !is.na(poverty_tier)) |>
  group_by(year, poverty_tier) |>
  summarise(avg_real_income = mean(median_income_2024_dollars, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
```

# Overview

## Row {height=20%}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "CPI Increase (2009-2023)"
list(
  icon  = "graph-up-arrow",
  color = "danger",
  value = paste0("+", cpi_change_pct, "%")
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Avg. Real Income Growth (2009-2023)"
list(
  icon  = "currency-dollar",
  color = "success",
  value = scales::dollar(round(income_change, 0))
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Counties Rent-Burdened (2023)"
list(
  icon  = "house-exclamation",
  color = "warning",
  value = scales::percent(pct_burdened, accuracy = 0.1)
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Avg. Rent-to-Income Ratio (2023)"
list(
  icon  = "percent",
  color = "info",
  value = scales::percent(avg_burden_2023, accuracy = 0.1)
)
```

## Row {height=80%}

```{r}
#| title: "Inflation-Adjusted Income by Poverty Tier (2009-2023)"

p <- income_trend |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = year, y = avg_real_income,
    color = poverty_tier, group = poverty_tier,
    text = paste0(
      poverty_tier, "<br>",
      "Year: ", year, "<br>",
      "Avg Income: ", scales::dollar(round(avg_real_income, 0))
    )
  )) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = tier_colors, name = "Poverty Tier") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2009, 2012, 2015, 2017, 2019, 2021, 2023)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::dollar_format(scale = 1e-3, suffix = "K")) +
  labs(
    x       = "Year",
    y       = "Avg. Real Median Income (2024 $)",
    caption = "Source: U.S. Census ACS | BLS CPI-U"
  ) +
  theme_few(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") |>
  layout(legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.2))
```

```{r}
#| title: "Rent Burden vs. Income, U.S. Counties (2023)"

scatter_2023 <- county_fused |>
  filter(
    year == 2023,
    !is.na(median_monthly_rent_cost_2024_dollars),
    !is.na(median_income_2024_dollars),
    !is.na(prop_poverty)
  )

p2 <- scatter_2023 |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x     = median_income_2024_dollars,
    y     = median_monthly_rent_cost_2024_dollars,
    color = prop_poverty,
    text  = paste0(
      county_state, "<br>",
      "Income: ", scales::dollar(round(median_income_2024_dollars, 0)), "<br>",
      "Monthly Rent: ", scales::dollar(round(median_monthly_rent_cost_2024_dollars, 0)), "<br>",
      "Poverty Rate: ", scales::percent(prop_poverty, accuracy = 0.1)
    )
  )) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.2) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 0.30 / 12, intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed", color = "#C62828", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "C", labels = scales::percent, name = "Poverty Rate") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::dollar_format(scale = 1e-3, suffix = "K")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    x       = "Median Annual Income (2024 $)",
    y       = "Median Monthly Rent (2024 $)",
    caption = "Source: U.S. Census ACS | BLS CPI-U"
  ) +
  theme_few(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p2, tooltip = "text") |>
  layout(legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.2))
```

# Maps

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "County Poverty Rates (2023)"

leaflet(gg_map_2023, options = leafletOptions(preferCanvas = TRUE)) |>
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.PositronNoLabels) |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor    = ~pal_poverty(prop_poverty),
    fillOpacity  = 0.8,
    color        = "grey80",
    weight       = 0.4,
    smoothFactor = 0.5,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 1.5, color = "#333333", fillOpacity = 0.95, bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = ~lapply(paste0(
      "<b>", county_state, "</b><br>",
      "Poverty Rate: ", scales::percent(prop_poverty, accuracy = 0.1), "<br>",
      "Median Income: ", scales::dollar(median_income, accuracy = 1)
    ), htmltools::HTML)
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position  = "bottomright",
    pal       = pal_poverty,
    values    = ~prop_poverty,
    title     = "Poverty Rate",
    labFormat = labelFormat(suffix = "%", transform = function(x) round(x * 100, 1))
  )
```

```{r}
#| title: "Monthly Rent as Share of Income (2023)"

pal_burden <- colorNumeric(
  palette  = c("#E8F5E9", "#FFF176", "#B71C1C"),
  domain   = c(0, 0.60),
  na.color = "grey90"
)

leaflet(gg_map_2023, options = leafletOptions(preferCanvas = TRUE)) |>
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.PositronNoLabels) |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor    = ~pal_burden(pmin(rent_to_income_ratio, 0.60, na.rm = TRUE)),
    fillOpacity  = 0.8,
    color        = "grey80",
    weight       = 0.4,
    smoothFactor = 0.5,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 1.5, color = "#333333", fillOpacity = 0.95, bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = ~lapply(paste0(
      "<b>", county_state, "</b><br>",
      "Rent / Income: ", scales::percent(rent_to_income_ratio, accuracy = 0.1), "<br>",
      "Monthly Rent: ", scales::dollar(median_monthly_rent_cost, accuracy = 1), "<br>",
      "Median Income: ", scales::dollar(median_income, accuracy = 1)
    ), htmltools::HTML)
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position  = "bottomright",
    pal       = pal_burden,
    values    = ~pmin(rent_to_income_ratio, 0.60, na.rm = TRUE),
    title     = "Rent / Income<br>(capped at 60%)",
    labFormat = labelFormat(suffix = "%", transform = function(x) round(x * 100, 1))
  )
```

# CPI Trend

## Row {height=100%}

```{r}
#| title: "U.S. Consumer Price Index (2000-2024)"

cpi_plot <- cpi_yearly |>
  mutate(in_window = year >= 2009 & year <= 2023) |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x    = year,
    y    = annual_avg_cpi,
    text = paste0("Year: ", year, "<br>CPI: ", round(annual_avg_cpi, 2))
  )) +
  annotate("rect",
    xmin = 2009, xmax = 2023,
    ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
    fill = "#E8F4FD", alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = in_window), size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey60", "TRUE" = "#1565C0")) +
  annotate("label",
    x = 2016, y = 245,
    label = "+42% (2009-2023)",
    color = "#C62828", size = 4, fontface = "bold",
    fill = "white", label.size = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2000, 2024, by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    x       = "Year",
    y       = "Annual Average CPI",
    caption = "Source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, CPI-U"
  ) +
  theme_few(base_size = 14)

ggplotly(cpi_plot, tooltip = "text")
```
